title: Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier (CSHT) actions affected by Lump Sum Exit Scheme payments
body: "If you’ve received a payment under the Lump Sum Exit Scheme, you must repay it if you want to add any of the listed CSHT actions to your CSHT agreement.  You do not need to repay the lump sum if:   you retained up to 5 hectares (ha) of agricultural land the retained land is the only land you want to enter into the listed actions   If you received the lump sum as a partnership or limited company, you do not need to repay it if only some partners or shareholders left the business.      CSHT action code  CSHT action title     CAB19 Pollen and nectar flower mix   CAB2 Basic overwinter stubble   CAB5 Nesting plots for lapwing and stone curlew   CAB6 Enhanced overwinter stubble   CAB7 Whole crop cereals   CAB18 Flower-rich grass margins, blocks, or in-field strips (on arable land or permanent crops)   CAB17 Winter bird food on arable and horticultural land   CAB10 Unharvested cereal headland   CAB11 Cultivated areas for arable plants   CAB12 Supplementary winter bird food   CAB14 Low input harvested cereal crops   CAB16 Bumblebird mix   CHRW4 Manage hedgerows   CCT3 Manage coastal saltmarsh and its vegetation   CCT4 Create intertidal and saline habitat on arable land   CCT5 Create intertidal and saline habitat by non-intervention   CCT7 Create intertidal and saline habitat on intensive grassland   VED1 Educational access visits   CGS26 Manage grassland with very low inputs   CGS4 Herbal leys   CGS22 Manage priority habitat species-rich grassland   CGS20 Manage wet grassland for breeding waders   CGS19 Manage wet grassland for wintering waders and wildfowl   CGS21 Manage grassland for target habitats, species or features   CHS2 Take historic and archaeological features out of cultivation   CHS3 Reduce cultivation depths on historic and archaeological features   CHS4 Control scrub on historic and archaeological features   CHS5 Manage historic and archaeological features on grassland   CHS7 Manage historic water meadows through traditional irrigation   CHS9 Restrict crop establishment depth on historic and archaeological features   CLH1 Manage lowland heathland   CLH3 Create lowland heathland   CAB2 Basic overwintered stubble   CAB17 Winter bird food on arable and horticulture land   COP3 Supplementary winter bird food (organic land)   CSW7 Arable reversion to grassland with low fertiliser input   CSW8 Manage intensive grassland adjacent to a watercourse   CSW12 Make room for the river to move   CSW13 Manage grassland to reduce nutrient levels in groundwater   CSW15 Flood mitigation on arable reversion to grassland   CSW16 Flood mitigation on permanent grassland   CSW17 Raise water levels in cropped or arable peat soils to near the land surface   CSW18 Raise water levels in permanent grassland peat soils to near the land surface   CUP2 Manage rough grazing for birds   CUP3 General moorland Management   CWD3 Manage woodland edges on arable land   CWD22 Manage wood pasture and parkland   CWD21 Restore wood pasture and parkland   CWD8 Create scrub and open habitat mosaics   CWT1 Buffer in-field ponds and ditches on improved grassland   CWT2 Buffer in-field ponds and ditches on arable land   CGS18 Manage species-rich floodplain meadows   CUP8 Moderate livestock grazing on moorland   CUP9 Low livestock grazing on moorland   CUP10 Limited livestock grazing on moorland   CUP11 Shepherding livestock on moorland (no required stock removal period)   CUP12 Shepherding livestock...
